briefdescription.para: Core C++ implementations for graph-based analysis.

detaileddescription.para: The Core module contains the fundamental C++ implementations that form the backbone of the AnalysisG framework. These classes provide the low-level graph structures, node and edge representations, and essential algorithms.

sect1.title: Overview
sect1.para: The core module implements the basic building blocks for graph-based physics analysis:

listitem.para: Graph: The main container for nodes and edges, representing the graph structure
Node: Represents individual vertices in the graph (typically particles)
Edge: Represents connections between nodes (typically particle relationships)

sect1.title: Files
sect1.para: The core module consists of the following source files:

listitem.para: graph.cxx: Implementation of graph structures and operations

sect1.title: Usage
sect1.para: The core classes are typically not used directly by end users. Instead, they are wrapped by the higher-level template classes in the modules directory:

listitem.para: Use event_template for event data handling

sect1.title: Design Principles
sect1.para: The core module follows these design principles:

listitem.para: Efficiency: Optimized for handling large numbers of events and particles
Flexibility: Designed to accommodate various graph topologies
Integration: Seamless integration with PyTorch tensors for ML workflows
Memory Management: Careful handling of dynamic allocations
